lyra 0.0.0.930
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Pad a String With Justification

Source: R/strPad.R
strPad.Rd

strPad will fill a string x with defined characters to fit a given length.

Usage

strPad(x, width = NULL, pad = " ", adj = "left")

Arguments

x

a vector of strings to be padded.

width

resulting width of padded string. If x is a vector and width is left to NULL, it will be set to the length of the largest string in x.

pad

string to pad with. Will be repeated as often as necessary. Default is " ".

adj

adjustement of the old string, one of "left", "right", "center". If set to "left" the old string will be adjusted on the left and the new characters will be filled in on the right side.

Value

the string

Details

If a string x has more characters than width, it will be chopped on the length of width.

See also

Other string: mgsub(), strAbbr(), strAlign(), strCap(), strChop(), strCountW(), strDist(), strExtract(), strExtractBetween(), strIsNumeric(), strLen(), strPos(), strRev(), strSpell(), strSplit(), strTrim(), strTrunc(), strVal()

Examples


strPad("My string", 25, "XoX", "center")
#> [1] "XoXXoXXoMy stringXoXXoXXo"
 # [1] "XoXXoXXoMy stringXXoXXoXX"
